sme_conditions.note: Both conditions cumulative; total assets exclude the land on which the business's office, plant, and equipment are situated.

cit.basis: 25% standard corporate income tax under the CREATE Act (Republic Act No. 11534); 20% for qualifying SMEs (net taxable income ≤ PHP 5,000,000 and total assets ≤ PHP 100,000,000 excluding land of the business site).

mcit.note: Minimum corporate income tax (MCIT) of 2% of gross income applies from the fourth taxable year following commencement of operations, when higher than the regular CIT.

cit.note: Administered by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR). The CREATE MORE Act (Republic Act No. 12066) kept the 25%/20% rates and set a 20% RCIT for registered business enterprises under the enhanced deductions regime — out of scope for this SME estimator.

wht.note: Dividends to non-resident foreign corporations: 25% final withholding tax, reduced to 15% under the tax-sparing rule (or lower treaty rates).

vat.note: 12% value-added tax on sales of goods and services; zero-rating and exemptions apply to certain transactions (e.g. exports).

personal_income_tax.note: Graduated personal income tax of 0-35% under the TRAIN Law (Republic Act No. 10963); first PHP 250,000 of annual taxable income is exempt.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
MCIT, incentives for registered business enterprises, WHT, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
